nodes.label: AI as writing assistant preserving personal voice
nodes.label_negative: AI writing that loses personal voice and sounds like a sales pitch
nodes.grounding_span: I'll have a goal and target audience in mind. Since I also used the AI model to help create my website, it has a good idea about my voice and personality, so it keeps things consistent.

nodes.label: AI as tool for market and business perspective
nodes.label_negative: AI limited to task execution without strategic insight
nodes.grounding_span: After finishing the blog post, the AI's follow-up suggested offering editing services to realtors who didn't hire a photographer. This led to a conversation that resulted in offering a new service and a new revenue stream.

nodes.label: AI for cut-and-dry objective questions
nodes.label_negative: AI for subjective tasks requiring personal vision
nodes.grounding_span: The financial tasks are cut and dried. They're either right or their wrong. My voice and vision don't matter in those tasks.

nodes.label: AI memory reliability in long conversations
nodes.label_negative: AI without memory requiring repetitive stage-setting
nodes.grounding_span: the tipping point was when the AI models started 'remembering' past conversations. It's very helpful when the model knows what I've tried, what I've dismissed, my voice and my goals.

nodes.label: AI as honest constructive critic
nodes.label_negative: Relying solely on own judgment without external input
nodes.grounding_span: AI has helped put me back on path before by explaining that even though the photo is good, it may not be what a realtor looking for a photographer is looking for.

nodes.label: Authentic personal expression
nodes.grounding_span: It's [REDACTED] photography I'm selling, not AI's.

nodes.label: Business growth and profitability
nodes.grounding_span: This led to a conversation that resulted in offering a new service and a new revenue stream.

nodes.label: Efficiency and accuracy in analytical tasks
nodes.grounding_span: The financial tasks are cut and dried. They're either right or their wrong.

nodes.label: Seamless workflow integration
nodes.grounding_span: The conversation can be productive from the first prompt in a new conversation. There's no stage-setting involved.

nodes.label: Objective validation and course correction
nodes.grounding_span: AI has helped put me back on path before by explaining that even though the photo is good, it may not be what a realtor looking for a photographer is looking for.

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for brainstorming
nodes.grounding_span: The most satisfying experience using AI came when I was writing a blog post...the AI's follow-up suggested offering editing services...This led to...a new revenue stream.

nodes.label: Cautious approach to AI in financial tasks
nodes.grounding_span: if it passes the smell test, I let it go. I'm always interested in learning and building new skills, but there are some things I admit AI does better and I trust it more and more to take over.

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI as memory aid
nodes.grounding_span: the tipping point was when the AI models started 'remembering' past conversations. It's very helpful...

nodes.label: Preference for collaborative AI use
nodes.grounding_span: Currently it's almost exclusively collaborative. When I learn to use it more, or trust the results more, I will likely delegate things like creating the P&L, tracking expenses, etc.

nodes.label: Collaborative iterative refinement — works back and forth with AI
nodes.grounding_span: I'll have a goal and target audience in mind...it will provide an email or description and I'll ask for tweaks before using.

nodes.label: Domain-specific delegation — uses AI for brainstorming but retains personalization
nodes.grounding_span: The financial tasks are cut and dried...My voice and vision don't matter in those tasks.
